content:
![Featured Image](https://media.tmpnews.com/images/Breaking-News.webp)

“I am feeling excited because we are voting in a free manner after 17 years,” Mohammed Jobair Hossain, 39, said as he waited in line at the polls.

“Our votes will matter and have meaning”, he told the Reuters news agency.

In addition to electing a new parliament, voters also voted in a referendum on the so‑called “July Charter” – a document that emerged from the 2024 uprising and proposes far-reaching constitutional reforms.
headline: ‘Feeling excited’: Voters feel empowered as elections mark a new beginning

content:
![Featured Image](https://media.tmpnews.com/images/Breaking-News.webp)

Nahid Islam, the chief of the National Citizen Party, has cast his vote at the AKM Rahmatullah College in Badda, the Dhaka neighbourhood where he was born and raised.

Islam said he had been of voting age since the 2018 election, but did not vote in either of the two national polls held under Sheikh Hasina’s government.

“I have cast my first vote in life without any hassle. I urge everybody to cast their vote,” he told reporters after voting.

“If people are allowed to vote freely and fairly, we are on the verge of forming the government,” he added. “We are hopeful of victory and will accept the people’s mandate.”

Islam is contesting from the Dhaka-11 constituency as part of the Jamaat-e-Islami 11-party alliance. His National Citizen Party was born out of the anti-Hasina protest movement.

Source:- aljazeera
headline: Nahid Islam casts his ballot

![Featured Image](https://i.ibb.co/9HyRR4nz/IMG-20260209-220139-695.jpg)

Retired Israeli Major General Yitzhak Brik warned Sunday that Israel could collapse before its 100th anniversary in 2048, citing deep internal divisions and growing international isolation. Writing in the daily Maariv, Brik described a society “torn from within,” riven by hatred between political factions, and led by a government he called “myopic and directionless.”

Brik highlighted Israel’s deteriorating global standing, noting that the country increasingly provokes revulsion abroad while emigration surges at home. He said the nation’s resilience is eroding across every sector, from security and the economy to health care and infrastructure, and called for a new generation of leaders to transform polarization into responsible governance.

The warning comes amid nearly two years of Israel’s genocide in Gaza, alongside ongoing attacks and acts of military aggression against Lebanon, Syria, Iran, Yemen, and Qatar. Brik framed these crises as symptomatic of a state in decline, cautioning that only decisive leadership and generational change could avert a collapse.
headline: Retired Israeli general warns that Israel could collapse before its 100th anniversary

US military airlift operations to the Middle East are continuing overnight, with several C-17 Globemaster transports and at least one C-5M Super Galaxy heavy cargo aircraft reportedly delivering additional air defense systems and strike support equipment. 

Meanwhile, an aerial refueling tanker departed Morón Air Base in Spain, likely escorting the final two EA-18G Growler electronic warfare jets toward Qatar.

![Featured Image](https://i.ibb.co/6ch7PYtr/New-Project-274-71-C5-A4-B.png)

Tehran, – President Masoud Pezeshkian says recent Iran-US talks, held with the follow-up of friendly regional governments, marked “a step forward,” stressing that Iran’s nuclear logic is based on rights enshrined in the Non-Proliferation Treaty.

In a message posted on X on Sunday, Pezeshkian wrote that “dialogue has always been our strategy for peaceful settlement,” adding that “our logic in the nuclear issue is the rights enshrined in the Non-Proliferation Treaty.”

He underlined that the Iranian nation “has always responded to respect with respect, but does not tolerate the language of force.”

The new round of indirect nuclear talks between Iran and the US began on Friday morning in Muscat with the mediation of the Sultanate of Oman and concluded several hours later.

During the talks, the Iranian and US negotiating teams conveyed a set of views, considerations, and approaches to one another through Oman’s foreign minister.
headline: Pezeshkian: Iran’s nuclear logic based on rights enshrined in NPT

 Tehran, – Iran has unveiled 63 new domestically-produced pharmaceutical items, including 19 first-time formulations, a move projected to save the country between $25 and $30 million annually in crucial foreign currency by reducing reliance on imported medicines and raw materials.

Speaking at the launch ceremony in Tehran, Mohammad Naderi Alizadeh said on Sunday that Iran’s Tamin Pharmaceutical Investment Holding (TIPICO) is one of the few holdings in the country that controls the full pharmaceutical value chain, from R&D and API production to manufacturing, distribution, and marketing.

The group operates 24 subsidiaries and three affiliated companies nationwide and produces 549 pharmaceutical products, covering most therapeutic needs in Iran.

He added that the company holds 25–30% of Iran’s drug distribution market and supplies medicines to over 40% of medical universities, many serving low‑income populations, despite large accumulated receivables in the health sector.

According to the CEO, TIPICO has eliminated all loss‑making subsidiaries and significantly improved financial performance. Cumulative profits of its finished‑product companies rose 138% in the first ten months of the year (March 21, 2025 to January 20, 2026), while total group profits more than doubled.

Of the newly unveiled products, 10 are APIs, 46 finished medicines and supplements, and seven veterinary drugs, generating $25–30 million in annual foreign‑currency savings.

Strategic products, including type‑2 diabetes and weight‑loss medicines and insulin glargine, are expected to enter the market next year after regulatory approval.
headline: Iran saves $30 million in foreign currency with 63 new pharmaceutical products

headline:
Iran has suspended visa-free entry for Indian nationals for travel and transit. 

From 19th November, Indian nationals with passports will now be required to obtain a visa to enter or transit through the territory of the Islamic Republic of Iran: Iran Statement. 

Indian nationals previously had the privilege of entering Iran without a visa for 15 days, a privilege that has now been revoked.

NDTV: At least 42 people are feared dead after a bus carrying Umrah pilgrims collided with a diesel tanker in Saudi Arabia early Sunday. 

Most victims were Indian nationals from Hyderabad, including women and children, and the bus was reportedly returning from Mecca when it caught fire.
